name: Snyk API
what_it_does: Snyk's REST API for programmatically accessing vulnerability scanning results for open-source dependencies, container images, infrastructure-as-code, and application code.
use_cases: Querying vulnerability reports for dependencies across projects
Integrating security scan results into CI/CD pipeline dashboards
Automating issue triage and prioritization workflows
Monitoring license compliance across open-source dependencies
Building security posture reports for engineering and security teams
not_for: Runtime application security monitoring (use Snyk's runtime product)
Teams without Snyk subscriptions (free tier very limited)
Network security or SIEM use cases
best_when: You need to programmatically access and act on dependency vulnerability data integrated into developer workflows.
avoid_when: You need runtime security monitoring or comprehensive DAST/penetration testing capabilities.

auth.notes: Personal API tokens and service account tokens. OAuth2 for partner/app integrations. Tokens scoped to organization. New REST API uses Bearer tokens; legacy v1 API also supported.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 200 open-source tests/month, limited container scans
pricing.notes: Free tier available with usage limits. API access available on all plans. Business/Enterprise for more test types and features. Pricing per developer seat.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors include status codes, error keys, and human-readable messages. New REST API (v3) is better documented than legacy v1.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All primary data endpoints are read-only GET operations — fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Two API versions (v1 legacy and new REST v3) with different auth and response shapes — use new REST API for new integrations
Organization ID required for most calls — fetch from /orgs endpoint first
Test endpoints (triggering new scans) count against monthly test quotas
Snyk's vulnerability database updates continuously — results can differ between sequential scans
Free tier test limits are per-user, not per-org — test the actual limit for your plan
